revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=川田神社| 所在地=滋賀県甲賀市水口町北内貴490| 祭神=[[天湯川桁命]]
[[天川田奈命]]| 社格=式内社（名神大）
県社| 創建=[[垂仁天皇]]御宇| 本殿=一間社[[流造]]| 例祭=[[4月25日]]| 神事=古式揚矢祭}} '''川田神社'''（かわたじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[甲賀市]]水口町に鎮座する[[神社]]である。[[垂仁天皇]]の御宇に創祀されたと伝わる。延喜式に記載された[[名神大社]]である。[[享保]]11年には正一位の神階を受けた。[[明治]]9年に[[村社]]、明治17年に[[郷社]]、[[昭和]]11年に[[県社]]に列した。 == 祭神 == * [[天湯川桁命]] * [[天川田奈命]] 〈配祀神〉 * [[天児屋根命]] * [[大己貴命]] == 神紋 == :下り藤 == 祭り == * 例祭 :4月25日に行われる。 * 古式揚矢祭 == 境内社 == * 天満宮 == 関連項目 == * [[神道]] * [[神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:かわたしんしやこうかし}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:甲賀市]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 川田神社 |画像 = [[Image:Kawata-jinja-1.JPG|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 滋賀県甲賀市水口町北内貴490 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|37.7|N|136|09|21.1|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[天湯川桁命]]
[[天川田奈命]] |社格 = 式内社（名神大）論社・旧県社 |創建 = 伝垂仁天皇朝 |本殿 = [[流造|一間社流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月25日]] |神事 = 古式揚矢祭（2月20日） }} '''川田神社'''（かわたじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[甲賀市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 == 祭神 == * [[天湯川桁命]] * [[天川田奈命]] 〈配祀神〉 * [[天児屋根命]] * [[大己貴命]] == 神紋 == *下り藤 == 由緒 == [[垂仁天皇]]の時代に創祀されたと伝わる。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載された[[名神大社]]の[[論社]]。[[享保]]11年（[[1726年]]）には正一位の[[神階]]を受けた。[[蔵田荘]]の[[惣社]]。[[明治]]9年（[[1876年]]）に[[村社]]、同17年（[[1884年]]）に[[郷社]]、[[昭和]]11年（[[1936年]]）に[[県社]]に列した。 == 祭り == * 例祭 :4月25日に行われる。 * 古式揚矢祭（ようやまつり） == 境内社 == * 天満宮 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=564 川田神社]（滋賀県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわたしんしや こうかし}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名かわたしんしや こうかし]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:甲賀市]]

旧[[県社]] |創建 = （伝）[[垂仁天皇]]朝 |本殿 = [[流造|一間社流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月25日]] |神事 = 古式揚矢祭（2月20日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=川田神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''川田神社'''（かわたじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[甲賀市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 == 祭神 == * [[天湯河板挙|天湯川桁命]] - 鳥取連の祖神 * 天川田奈命 〈配祀神〉 * [[天児屋根命]] * [[大己貴命]] == 神紋 == *下り藤 == 由緒 == [[垂仁天皇]]の時代に創祀されたと伝わる。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載された[[名神大社]]の[[論社]]。[[享保]]11年（[[1726年]]）には正一位の[[神階]]を受けた。[[蔵田荘]]の[[惣社]]。[[明治]]9年（[[1876年]]）に[[村社]]、同17年（[[1884年]]）に[[郷社]]、[[昭和]]11年（[[1936年]]）に[[県社]]に列した。 == 祭り == * 例祭 :4月25日に行われる。 * 古式揚矢祭（ようやまつり） == 境内社 == * 天満宮 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=564 川田神社]（滋賀県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわたしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名かわたしんしや]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:甲賀市の建築物]]

{{nihongo|'''Kawata Shrine (Koka)'''|川田神社 (甲賀市水口町北内貴)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kawata Shrine (Koka)'''|川田神社 (甲賀市水口町北内貴)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kawata Shrine (Koka)'''|川田神社 (甲賀市水口町北内貴)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kawata Shrine (Koka)'''|川田神社 (甲賀市水口町北内貴)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kawata Shrine (Koka)'''|川田神社 (甲賀市水口町北内貴)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kawata Shrine (Koka)'''|川田神社 (甲賀市水口町北内貴)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kawata Shrine (Koka)'''|川田神社 (甲賀市水口町北内貴)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kawata Shrine (Koka)'''|川田神社 (甲賀市水口町北内貴)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kawata Shrine (Koka)'''|川田神社 (甲賀市水口町北内貴)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kawata Shrine (Koka)'''|川田神社 (甲賀市水口町北内貴)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kawata Shrine (Koka)'''|川田神社 (甲賀市水口町北内貴)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kawata Shrine (Koka)'''|川田神社 (甲賀市水口町北内貴)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kawata Shrine (Koka)'''|川田神社 (甲賀市水口町北内貴)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]].

拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|滋賀県|Q131358}}{{jalink|甲賀市|Q847584}}{{jalink|水口町|Q3314706}}北内貴490 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|37.7|N|136|09|21.1|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|天湯河板挙|Q52319907|天湯川桁命}}
天川田奈命 |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964|名神大}}）論社
旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = （伝）{{jalink|垂仁天皇|Q314850}}朝 |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684|一間社流造}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|4月25日|Q2531}} |神事 = 古式揚矢祭（2月20日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=川田神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''川田神社'''（かわたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|滋賀県|Q131358}}{{jalink|甲賀市|Q847584}}に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。 == 祭神 == * {{jalink|天湯河板挙|Q52319907|天湯川桁命}} - 鳥取連の祖神 * 天川田奈命 〈配祀神〉 * {{jalink|天児屋根命}} * {{jalink|大己貴命}} == 神紋 == *下り藤 == 由緒 == {{jalink|垂仁天皇|Q314850}}の時代に創祀されたと伝わる。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に記載された{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}の{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}。{{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}11年（{{jalink|1726年|Q6936}}）には正一位の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}を受けた。{{jalink|蔵田荘}}の{{jalink|惣社}}。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}9年（{{jalink|1876年|Q7766}}）に{{jalink|村社}}、同17年（{{jalink|1884年|Q7819}}）に{{jalink|郷社}}、{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}11年（{{jalink|1936年|Q18649}}）に{{jalink|県社}}に列した。 == 祭り == * 例祭 :4月25日に行われる。 * 古式揚矢祭（ようやまつり） == 境内社 == * 天満宮 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=564 川田神社]（滋賀県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名かわたしんしや]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:甲賀市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

Amakawata-na-no-Mikoto |Shrine Rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|Myōjin Great Shrine}}) candidate shrine
Former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |Founded = (Traditionally) reign of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}} |Main Hall = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|Ichiken-sha nagare-zukuri}} |Other Names= |Pilgrimage Status= |Annual Festival = {{jalink|April 25|Q2531}} |Rituals = Ancient-style Yōya Festival (February 20) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kawata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawata Shrine''' (かわたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Kōka City|Q847584}}, {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}. === Enshrined Deities === * {{jalink|Ametoyukawa Itage|Q52319907|Ametoyukawa-geta-no-Mikoto}} – ancestral deity of the Tottori no Muraji clan * Amakawata-na-no-Mikoto 〈Associated Deities〉 * {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} === Shrine Crest === * Descending wisteria === History === It is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}}. It is a {{jalink|candidate shrine|Q135022904}} of a {{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}. In {{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} 11 ({{jalink|1726|Q6936}}), it was granted the divine rank of Senior First Rank ({{jalink|shinkai|Q11591025}}). It served as the {{jalink|sōsha}} of {{jalink|Kurata Estate}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 9 ({{jalink|1876|Q7766}}) it was designated a {{jalink|Village Shrine}}, in the same era’s 17th year ({{jalink|1884|Q7819}}) a {{jalink|Township Shrine}}, and in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 11 ({{jalink|1936|Q18649}}) it was promoted to a {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. === Festivals === * Annual Festival : Held on April 25. * Ancient-style Yōya Festival === Auxiliary Shrines === * Tenmangū === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=564 Kawata Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名かわたしんしや]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:甲賀市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

It served as the {{jalink|sōsha}} of {{jalink|Kurata Estate}}. Worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Kōka City|Q847584}}{{jalink|Minakuchi Town|Q3314706}} Kita-Uchiaki 490 |Coordinates= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|37.7|N|136|09|21.1|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined Deities = {{jalink|Ametoyukawa Itage|Q52319907|Ametoyukawa-geta-no-Mikoto}}
Amakawata-na-no-Mikoto |Shrine Rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|Myōjin Great Shrine}}) candidate shrine
Former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |Founded = (Traditionally) reign of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}} |Main Hall = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|Ichiken-sha nagare-zukuri}} |Other Names= |Pilgrimage Status= |Annual Festival = {{jalink|April 25|Q2531}} |Rituals = Ancient-style Yōya Festival (February 20) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kawata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawata Shrine''' (かわたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Kōka City|Q847584}}, {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}. === Enshrined Deities === * {{jalink|Ametoyukawa Itage|Q52319907|Ametoyukawa-geta-no-Mikoto}} – ancestral deity of the Tottori no Muraji clan * Amakawata-na-no-Mikoto 〈Associated Deities〉 * {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} === Shrine Crest === * Descending wisteria === History === It is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}}. It is a {{jalink|candidate shrine|Q135022904}} of a {{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}. In {{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} 11 ({{jalink|1726|Q6936}}), it was granted the divine rank of Senior First Rank ({{jalink|shinkai|Q11591025}}). It served as the {{jalink|sōsha}} of {{jalink|Kurata Estate}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 9 ({{jalink|1876|Q7766}}) it was designated a {{jalink|Village Shrine}}, in the same era’s 17th year ({{jalink|1884|Q7819}}) a {{jalink|Township Shrine}}, and in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 11 ({{jalink|1936|Q18649}}) it was promoted to a {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. === Festivals === * Annual Festival : Held on April 25. * Ancient-style Yōya Festival === Auxiliary Shrines === * Tenmangū === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=564 Kawata Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名かわたしんしや]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:甲賀市の建築物]]

Former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |Founded = (Traditionally) reign of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}} |Main Hall = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|Ichiken-sha nagare-zukuri}} |Other Names= |Pilgrimage Status= |Annual Festival = {{jalink|April 25|Q2531}} |Rituals = Ancient-style Yōya Festival (February 20) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kawata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawata Shrine''' (かわたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Kōka City|Q847584}}, {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}. === Enshrined Deities === * {{jalink|Ametoyukawa Itage|Q52319907|Ametoyukawa-geta-no-Mikoto}} – ancestral deity of the Tottori no Muraji clan * Amakawata-na-no-Mikoto 〈Associated Deities〉 * {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} === Shrine Crest === * Descending wisteria === History === It is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}}. It is a {{jalink|candidate shrine|Q135022904}} of a {{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}. In {{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} 11 ({{jalink|1726|Q6936}}), it was granted the divine rank of Senior First Rank ({{jalink|shinkai|Q11591025}}). It served as the {{jalink|sōsha}} of {{jalink|Kurata Estate}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 9 ({{jalink|1876|Q7766}}) it was designated a {{jalink|Village Shrine}}, in the same era’s 17th year ({{jalink|1884|Q7819}}) a {{jalink|Township Shrine}}, and in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 11 ({{jalink|1936|Q18649}}) it was promoted to a {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. === Festivals === * Annual Festival : Held on April 25. * Ancient-style Yōya Festival === Auxiliary Shrines === * Tenmangū === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=564 Kawata Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名かわたしんしや]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:甲賀市の建築物]]

Worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Kōka City|Q847584}}{{jalink|Minakuchi Town|Q3314706}} Kita-Uchiaki 490 |Coordinates= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|37.7|N|136|09|21.1|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined Deities = {{jalink|Ametoyukawa Itage|Q52319907|Ametoyukawa-geta-no-Mikoto}}
Amakawata-na-no-Mikoto |Shrine Rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|Myōjin Great Shrine}}) candidate shrine
Former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |Founded = (Traditionally) reign of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}} |Main Hall = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|Ichiken-sha nagare-zukuri}} |Other Names= |Pilgrimage Status= |Annual Festival = {{jalink|April 25|Q2531}} |Rituals = Ancient-style Yōya Festival (February 20) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kawata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawata Shrine''' (かわたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Kōka City|Q847584}}, {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}. === Enshrined Deities === * {{jalink|Ametoyukawa Itage|Q52319907|Ametoyukawa-geta-no-Mikoto}} – ancestral deity of the Tottori no Muraji clan * Amakawata-na-no-Mikoto 〈Associated Deities〉 * {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} === Shrine Crest === * Descending wisteria === History === It is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}}. It is a {{jalink|candidate shrine|Q135022904}} of a {{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}. In {{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} 11 ({{jalink|1726|Q6936}}), it was granted the divine rank of Senior First Rank ({{jalink|shinkai|Q11591025}}). It served as the {{jalink|sōsha}} of {{jalink|Kurata Estate}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 9 ({{jalink|1876|Q7766}}) it was designated a {{jalink|Village Shrine}}, in the same era’s 17th year ({{jalink|1884|Q7819}}) a {{jalink|Township Shrine}}, and in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 11 ({{jalink|1936|Q18649}}) it was promoted to a {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. === Festivals === * Annual Festival : Held on April 25. * Ancient-style Yōya Festival === Auxiliary Shrines === * Tenmangū === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=564 Kawata Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名かわたしんしや]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:甲賀市の建築物]]

Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|川田神社 (甲賀市水口町北内貴)|version=107300391|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Kawata Shrine |Image = [[Image:Kawata-jinja-1.JPG|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Kōka City|Q847584}}{{jalink|Minakuchi Town|Q3314706}} Kita-Uchiaki 490 |Coordinates= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|37.7|N|136|09|21.1|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined Deities = {{jalink|Ametoyukawa Itage|Q52319907|Ametoyukawa-geta-no-Mikoto}}
Amakawata-na-no-Mikoto |Shrine Rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|Myōjin Great Shrine}}) candidate shrine
Former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |Founded = (Traditionally) reign of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}} |Main Hall = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|Ichiken-sha nagare-zukuri}} |Other Names= |Pilgrimage Status= |Annual Festival = {{jalink|April 25|Q2531}} |Rituals = Ancient-style Yōya Festival (February 20) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kawata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawata Shrine''' (かわたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Kōka City|Q847584}}, {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}. === Enshrined Deities === * {{jalink|Ametoyukawa Itage|Q52319907|Ametoyukawa-geta-no-Mikoto}} – ancestral deity of the Tottori no Muraji clan * Amakawata-na-no-Mikoto 〈Associated Deities〉 * {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} === Shrine Crest === * Descending wisteria === History === It is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}}. It is a {{jalink|candidate shrine|Q135022904}} of a {{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}. In {{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} 11 ({{jalink|1726|Q6936}}), it was granted the divine rank of Senior First Rank ({{jalink|shinkai|Q11591025}}). It served as the {{jalink|sōsha}} of {{jalink|Kurata Estate}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 9 ({{jalink|1876|Q7766}}) it was designated a {{jalink|Village Shrine}}, in the same era’s 17th year ({{jalink|1884|Q7819}}) a {{jalink|Township Shrine}}, and in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 11 ({{jalink|1936|Q18649}}) it was promoted to a {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. === Festivals === * Annual Festival : Held on April 25. * Ancient-style Yōya Festival === Auxiliary Shrines === * Tenmangū === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=564 Kawata Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名かわたしんしや]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:甲賀市の建築物]]

{{Shrine |Name = Kawata Shrine |Image = [[Image:Kawata-jinja-1.JPG|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Kōka City|Q847584}}{{jalink|Minakuchi Town|Q3314706}} Kita-Uchiaki 490 |Coordinates= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|37.7|N|136|09|21.1|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined Deities = {{jalink|Ametoyukawa Itage|Q52319907|Ametoyukawa-geta-no-Mikoto}}
Amakawata-na-no-Mikoto |Shrine Rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|Myōjin Great Shrine}}) candidate shrine
Former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |Founded = (Traditionally) reign of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}} |Main Hall = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|Ichiken-sha nagare-zukuri}} |Other Names= |Pilgrimage Status= |Annual Festival = {{jalink|April 25|Q2531}} |Rituals = Ancient-style Yōya Festival (February 20) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kawata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawata Shrine''' (かわたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Kōka City|Q847584}}, {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}. === Enshrined Deities === * {{jalink|Ametoyukawa Itage|Q52319907|Ametoyukawa-geta-no-Mikoto}} – ancestral deity of the Tottori no Muraji clan * Amakawata-na-no-Mikoto 〈Associated Deities〉 * {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} === Shrine Crest === * Descending wisteria === History === It is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}}. It is a {{jalink|candidate shrine|Q135022904}} of a {{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}. In {{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} 11 ({{jalink|1726|Q6936}}), it was granted the divine rank of Senior First Rank ({{jalink|shinkai|Q11591025}}). It served as the {{jalink|sōsha}} of {{jalink|Kurata Estate}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 9 ({{jalink|1876|Q7766}}) it was designated a {{jalink|Village Shrine}}, in the same era’s 17th year ({{jalink|1884|Q7819}}) a {{jalink|Township Shrine}}, and in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 11 ({{jalink|1936|Q18649}}) it was promoted to a {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. === Festivals === * Annual Festival : Held on April 25. * Ancient-style Yōya Festival === Auxiliary Shrines === * Tenmangū === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=564 Kawata Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名かわたしんしや]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:甲賀市の建築物]]

It is a {{jalink|candidate shrine|Q135022904}} of a {{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}. In {{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} 11 ({{jalink|1726|Q6936}}), it was granted the divine rank of Senior First Rank ({{jalink|shinkai|Q11591025}}). It served as the {{jalink|sōsha}} of {{jalink|Kurata Estate}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 9 ({{jalink|1876|Q7766}}) it was designated a {{jalink|Village Shrine}}, in the same era’s 17th year ({{jalink|1884|Q7819}}) a {{jalink|Township Shrine}}, and in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 11 ({{jalink|1936|Q18649}}) it was promoted to a {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. === Festivals === * Annual Festival : Held on April 25. * Ancient-style Yōya Festival === Auxiliary Shrines === * Tenmangū === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=564 Kawata Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名かわたしんしや]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Koka, Shiga]]

It is a {{jalink|candidate shrine|Q135022904}} of a {{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}. In {{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} 11 ({{jalink|1726|Q6936}}), it was granted the divine rank of Senior First Rank ({{jalink|shinkai|Q11591025}}). It served as the {{jalink|sōsha}} of {{jalink|Kurata Estate}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 9 ({{jalink|1876|Q7766}}) it was designated a {{jalink|Village Shrine}}, in the same era’s 17th year ({{jalink|1884|Q7819}}) a {{jalink|Township Shrine}}, and in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 11 ({{jalink|1936|Q18649}}) it was promoted to a {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. === Festivals === * Annual Festival : Held on April 25. * Ancient-style Yōya Festival === Auxiliary Shrines === * Tenmangū === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=564 Kawata Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名かわたしんしや]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Koka, Shiga]]

full ja history}}== Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Kawata Shrine |Image = [[Image:Kawata-jinja-1.JPG|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Kōka City|Q847584}}{{jalink|Minakuchi Town|Q3314706}} Kita-Uchiaki 490 |Coordinates= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|37.7|N|136|09|21.1|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined Deities = {{jalink|Ametoyukawa Itage|Q52319907|Ametoyukawa-geta-no-Mikoto}}
Amakawata-na-no-Mikoto |Shrine Rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|Myōjin Great Shrine}}) candidate shrine
Former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |Founded = (Traditionally) reign of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}} |Main Hall = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|Ichiken-sha nagare-zukuri}} |Other Names= |Pilgrimage Status= |Annual Festival = {{jalink|April 25|Q2531}} |Rituals = Ancient-style Yōya Festival (February 20) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kawata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawata Shrine''' (かわたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Kōka City|Q847584}}, {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}. === Enshrined Deities === * {{jalink|Ametoyukawa Itage|Q52319907|Ametoyukawa-geta-no-Mikoto}} – ancestral deity of the Tottori no Muraji clan * Amakawata-na-no-Mikoto 〈Associated Deities〉 * {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} === Shrine Crest === * Descending wisteria === History === It is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}}. It is a {{jalink|candidate shrine|Q135022904}} of a {{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}. In {{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} 11 ({{jalink|1726|Q6936}}), it was granted the divine rank of Senior First Rank ({{jalink|shinkai|Q11591025}}). It served as the {{jalink|sōsha}} of {{jalink|Kurata Estate}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 9 ({{jalink|1876|Q7766}}) it was designated a {{jalink|Village Shrine}}, in the same era’s 17th year ({{jalink|1884|Q7819}}) a {{jalink|Township Shrine}}, and in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 11 ({{jalink|1936|Q18649}}) it was promoted to a {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. === Festivals === * Annual Festival : Held on April 25. * Ancient-style Yōya Festival === Auxiliary Shrines === * Tenmangū === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=564 Kawata Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名かわたしんしや]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Koka, Shiga]]
